tag.note: Not independently re-executed as a separate run; git diff between release_commit and main_commit shows zero changes under src/ or tests/, so main's test results are representative of the tag's test surface.

main.note: Fails with 'Cannot import setuptools.build_meta' unless setuptools/wheel are explicitly installed first, matching the CI root cause.

content_equivalence.root_cause_of_hash_divergence: core.autocrlf=true with no .gitattributes causes CRLF vs LF variance across checkout environments; no functional content divergence.

release_commit.root_cause: Missing explicit setuptools/wheel install before 'python -m build --no-isolation'; fixed on main by adding 'pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel build' before the editable install.

word_gate.orphan_process_risk_detail: If DispatchEx never returns, word_pid is never captured, so timeout-triggered cleanup cannot identify/terminate the spawned WINWORD.EXE process; the worker subprocess itself is reliably killed.

findings.title: render-docx silently overwrites input file when --output equals --input
Footnote-free valid documents incorrectly fail structural validation
Orphaned WINWORD.EXE possible if DispatchEx hangs before returning
Dead schema-version-mismatch error path (load_state never called by real pipeline)
Implicit schema-directory override with no integrity check
generated_at manifest field is a hardcoded constant mislabeled as a real timestamp
Zip-bomb compression-ratio heuristic false-positives on legitimate long Arabic text
No CI workflow builds or publishes release assets; published wheel has no CI-verifiable provenance
Wheel builds are not byte-reproducible across checkout environments (CRLF/LF variance, no .gitattributes)
No output file-extension validation on render-docx
GitHub Actions pinned to mutable version tags, not commit SHAs
Dependencies floor-pinned only, no upper bound
Missing required dependency causes raw traceback instead of clean CLI error
No LICENSE file in a public repository

required_actions: Fix CLI-1: reject or warn when --output resolves to the same path as --input before writing.
Fix DOCX-1: do not require w:bidi on synthetic separator footnotes when no real footnotes exist.
Fix WORD-1: snapshot processes before DispatchEx and diff post-timeout to find orphaned winword.exe even when word_pid was never captured.
Publish a v0.3.1 patch release once CLI-1, DOCX-1, and WORD-1 are fixed.
Add a tag-triggered CI workflow that builds and publishes release assets, so future releases have CI-verifiable provenance.
Add a .gitattributes file pinning text sources to LF to make wheel builds byte-reproducible.
Do not force-move the v0.3.0 tag or rewrite published history.
Do not delete the v0.3.0 GitHub Release; supersede it with v0.3.1 instead.
limitations: Real Microsoft Word COM automation was not executed; Word-gate findings are based on static code review plus the project's own diagnostics evidence of a real DispatchEx hang.
Tag-specific test/coverage numbers were not independently re-executed as a separate pytest run; relied on a verified zero-diff in src/tests between the release commit and main.
No automated fuzzing, pip-audit, bandit, or semgrep scans were run; security review was manual grep-and-read only.
Symlink-escape scenarios were reasoned about via code tracing but not physically exploit-tested.
Exhaustive manual mutation testing (as suggested in the audit brief) was not performed beyond the adversarial DOCX/CLI fixtures actually exercised.

remediation.summary: 11 FIXED, 2 MITIGATED (WORD-1, REL-1), 1 DEFERRED (LIC-1, no license created on owner behalf).
